body:
{
  "title": "Keaton Bakker — Enterprise Advisor",
  "compiled_truth": "Keaton Bakker is an enterprise advisor at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor), brought on in mid-2026 to help the company navigate its first serious go-to-market push into large accounts. Before joining as an advisor, Keaton spent nearly a decade as VP of Enterprise Sales at a major cloud data platform, where he built a team of 80+ reps and personally oversaw expansion into financial services and healthcare verticals. He's known internally as the person who opened Anchor's first Fortune 500 conversation — a deal that ultimately became a proof-of-concept with a top-10 US bank.

Bakker's style is direct, sometimes blunt. He doesn't have patience for slide decks that bury the value prop, and he's been vocal about Anchor needing to tighten its messaging around deterministic data infrastructure before scaling outbound. In early advisor sessions he pushed hard on the idea that "single-pass primitives" needed to be translated into langauge that a CISO or VP of Data Engineering could repeat to their boss in one sentence. That feedback shaped a lot of the positioning work done in Q3 2026.

Beyond GTM, Keaton has been a quiet but consistent force in recruiting. He's referred at least three senior hires to [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor), including a solutions engineer who came from his former team. His network skews toward enterprise infrastructure buyers and sellers — exactly the kind of people Anchor needs as it moves upmarket. He typically joins the weekly GTM sync but not the full team standup, and his advisory commitment is roughly 5–8 hours per month.

One thing worth noting: Keaton is not a deeply technical person. He relies on the engineering team to validate feasability of customer asks, and he's said openly that he doesn't pretend to understand the codec layer. What he does understand is procurement cycles, champion-building, and how to structure a pilot so it converts. That's his lane and he stays in it. The team respects him for that clarity.

Bakker holds a small equity stake, vesting over two years from his advisory start date. He's based in Chicago but travels to the team's east coast office roughly once a quarter.",
  "timeline": "- **2026-05-14** | Keaton Bakker formally signs advisor agreement with [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor); equity grant begins vesting.
- **2026-06-02** | Opens first Fortune 500 conversation — intro to VP of Data Engineering at a major US bank through his personal network.
- **2026-08-19** | Leads internal workshop on enterprise messaging; pushes team to simplify positioning around single-pass primitives for non-technical buyers.
- **2026-10-11** | Refers a senior solutions engineer from his former cloud platform team; candidate accepts offer two weeks later.
- **2027-01-23** | Joins quarterly GTM review; flags that Anchor's pilot-to-close ratio is below industry benchmarks and recommends structured success criteria for all new POCs.
- **2027-04-07** | Accompanies founding team on-site visit to the Fortune 500 bank account; helps navigate procurement and legal review process.
- **2027-07-30** | Introduces Anchor to two additional enterprise prospects in the healthcare vertical, both sourced from his personal rolodex.
- **2027-11-15** | Participates in board-adjacent strategy session on whether to build a dedicated enterprise sales team or continue founder-led sales.
- **2028-02-22** | Refers a VP of Sales candidate who ultimately joins [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) as the company's first dedicated sales hire.
- **2028-06-10** | Advisory equity finishes vesting; Keaton agrees to continue in an informal capacity, attending monthly check-ins."
}
